metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_668
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fbxl20 ubiquitinates and degrades the active zone protein Rim-1 (Fig.2-4). Fbxl20 null mutant hippocampal neurons show increased spontaneous (mEPSC) frequency but not amplitude. The increased mEPSC frequency can be rescued to wild type levels by shRNA-mediated depletion of RIM-1. In hippocampal slices from Fbxl20 null mutant mice increased mini frequency can be rescued by blocking proteasome activity. Hence Fbxl20 modulates synaptic transmission by ubiquitination and degradation of Rim-1.

23/5/20176 Pim
- Experiments:
Fig.2 Fbxl20 binds SCF complex proteins involved in ubiquitination (co-IP, mouse brain), binds ubiquitinated proteins (co-IP overexpressed Fbxl20, heterologous cells), binds RIM1 (co-IP, mouse brain), colocalizes with RIM1 in neurons (conf.IF, cultured neurons), directly interacts with RIM1 (co-IP, cell free system), and mediates ubiquitination of RIM1 protein (co-IP, western blot, cell free system).
Fig.3 Levels of RIM1 protein are increased and levels of RIM1 ubiquitination are decreased in Fbxl20 KO mice (IP + western blot, genetic transformation).
Fig.4-7 Fbxl20 KO changes synaptic ultrastructure and SV release (Ephys, genetic transformation). Experimental description: M&M: "Primary cultured neurons from SCR-KO **mice** were prepared with P1- P3 mice".
